title: helleborine latifolia f. dentata
intro: Helleborine latifolia f. dentata, also known as the broad-leaved helleborine, is a subspecies of orchid native to Europe, Asia, and Africa. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been associated with various health benefits, such as reducing fever and treating respiratory issues, due to its expectorant and cough-suppressant properties. In herbal preparations, the roots and rhizomes of the helleborine latifolia f. dentata are typically dried and used in teas, tinctures, and infusions to harness its medicinal and therapeutic effects.
